org.docgene.help.gui.jfx

Class JFXHelpContentViewer

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.docgene.help.AbstractHelpContentViewer<javafx.scene.Node,javafx.stage.Stage>
      • org.docgene.help.gui.jfx.JFXHelpContentViewer
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    HelpContentViewer<javafx.scene.Node,javafx.stage.Stage>

    public class JFXHelpContentViewer
extends AbstractHelpContentViewer<javafx.scene.Node,javafx.stage.Stage>
    The JavaFX HelpContentViewer. The JFXHelpContentViewer allows to show the content of a help system in a Swing GUI. The steps to use to use the HelpContentViewer are:
    • Install the ArticlesModel by calling the installModel(org.docgene.help.model.ArticlesModel) method. Note that this can also be performed directly through the JavaHelpFactory by the JavaHelpFactory.install(org.docgene.help.HelpContentViewer) method
    • Get the JFXHelpContentViewer component: getHelpComponent() and add it in your GUI
    • Or alternatively get the HelpContentViewer dialog window: getHelpWindow(javafx.stage.Stage, java.lang.String, int, int) and add it in your GUI
    Adding context-sensitive help on components is performed through the registerContextHelp(java.lang.String, javafx.scene.Node) method.

      • registerContextHelp

        public boolean registerContextHelp(java.lang.String helpID,
                                   javafx.scene.Node node)
        Add a context sensitive help to a Node.
        Parameters:
        helpID - the helpRef id
        node - the Node
        Returns:
        true if the context sensitive help could be added. It will return false if no helpRef of the specified id exists

      • registerContextHelp

        public boolean registerContextHelp(java.lang.String helpID,
                                   javafx.scene.control.MenuItem item)
        Add a context sensitive help to a MenuItem. Note that for example, the context sensitive help will not be taken into account for MenuItems.
        Parameters:
        helpID - the helpRef id
        item - the MenuItem
        Returns:
        true if the context sensitive help could be added. It will return false if no helpRef of the specified id exists

      • registerContextHelpScene

        public void registerContextHelpScene(javafx.scene.Scene scene)
        Add a Scene managed by the context help.
        Parameters:
        scene - the Scene

      • registerContextHelpWindow

        public void registerContextHelpWindow(javafx.stage.Stage stage)
        Add a Stage managed by the context help. Call the underlying registerContextHelpScene(javafx.scene.Scene) method.
        Parameters:
        stage - the Stage

      • createContextHelpButton

        public javafx.scene.control.Button createContextHelpButton()
        Create a button to start a context sensitive help.
        Returns:
        the button component

      • showHelpDialog

        public void showHelpDialog(javafx.scene.Node relativeTo)
        Show the help dialog relative to a Node. This method is protected against cases where the component is not showing.
        Parameters:
        relativeTo - the Node from which the help Window must be relative to (can be null)

      • showHelpDialog

        public void showHelpDialog(int x,
                           int y)
        Show the help dialog.
        Parameters:
        x - the x position on screen
        y - the y position on screen

      • showHelpTopic

        public void showHelpTopic(java.lang.String helpID,
                          javafx.scene.Node relativeTo)
        Show the help topic for a specified id.
        Parameters:
        helpID - the help id
        relativeTo - the Node from which the help Window must be relative to (can be null)
